briefdescription.para: The Unicode character and combining code points.
The style for this character.
Construct an empty block character using inherited colors.
Construct a block character from a single Unicode code point using inherited colors.

detaileddescription.para:
Control codes (except new-line and tab) are replaced with a space - to represent an "invisible" character. Invalid Unicode code points are replaced with the Unicode replacement character.
codePoint
The base Unicode code point.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with inherited colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-8 encoded text to display. Invalid or unsupported text normalizes deterministically to a single renderable character. Empty input, control codes, and leading zero-width code points normalize to U+FFFD. Later visible code points also collapse the result to U+FFFD, while a third combining mark is ignored.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with inherited colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-32 encoded text to display. Invalid or unsupported text normalizes deterministically to a single renderable character. Empty input, control codes, invalid Unicode scalar values, and leading zero-width code points normalize to U+FFFD. Later visible code points also collapse the result to U+FFFD, while a third combining mark is ignored.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char32_t codePoint, const CharStyle style) noexcept
memberdef.name: Char

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character from a single Unicode code point with explicit style.

parameterdescription.para: The base Unicode code point.
The style for the character.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char32_t codePoint, const Color color, const CharAttributes attributes) noexcept
memberdef.name: Char

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character from a single Unicode code point with explicit color and attributes.

parameterdescription.para: The base Unicode code point.
The color for the character.
The character attributes.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with explicit text and style.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-8 encoded text to display.

The style for the character. Invalid or unsupported text normalizes deterministically to a single renderable character. Empty input, control codes, and leading zero-width code points normalize to U+FFFD. Later visible code points also collapse the result to U+FFFD, while a third combining mark is ignored.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string_view charStr, const Color color, const CharAttributes attributes) noexcept
memberdef.name: Char

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with explicit text, color, and attributes.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-8 encoded text to display.
The color for the character.

The character attributes. Invalid or unsupported text normalizes deterministically to a single renderable character. Empty input, control codes, and leading zero-width code points normalize to U+FFFD. Later visible code points also collapse the result to U+FFFD, while a third combining mark is ignored.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with explicit text and style.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-32 encoded text to display.

The style for the character. Invalid or unsupported text normalizes deterministically to a single renderable character. Empty input, control codes, invalid Unicode scalar values, and leading zero-width code points normalize to U+FFFD. Later visible code points also collapse the result to U+FFFD, while a third combining mark is ignored.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::u32string_view charStr, const Color color, const CharAttributes attributes) noexcept
memberdef.name: Char

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with explicit text, color, and attributes.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-32 encoded text to display.
The color for the character.

The character attributes. Invalid or unsupported text normalizes deterministically to a single renderable character. Empty input, control codes, invalid Unicode scalar values, and leading zero-width code points normalize to U+FFFD. Later visible code points also collapse the result to U+FFFD, while a third combining mark is ignored.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character from a single Unicode code point and a color.

parameterdescription.para: The base Unicode code point.
The color for the character.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with explicit text and colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-8 encoded text to display.

The color for the character. Invalid or unsupported text normalizes deterministically to a single renderable character. Empty input, control codes, and leading zero-width code points normalize to U+FFFD. Later visible code points also collapse the result to U+FFFD, while a third combining mark is ignored.

briefdescription.para: Construct a block character with explicit text and colors.

parameterdescription.para: The UTF-32 encoded text to display.

The color for the character. Invalid or unsupported text normalizes deterministically to a single renderable character. Empty input, control codes, invalid Unicode scalar values, and leading zero-width code points normalize to U+FFFD. Later visible code points also collapse the result to U+FFFD, while a third combining mark is ignored.

briefdescription.para: Compare two terminal characters for equality.
Compare two terminal characters for inequality.
Compare just a single-code point character, without the color.
Compare just a single-code point character, without the color.
Get the character string as UTF-8 text.

simplesect.para: A UTF-8 encoded copy of the stored character sequence.

briefdescription.para: Get the leading Unicode code point.

simplesect.para: The base code point, or 0 if this character is empty.

briefdescription.para: Get a single Unicode code point or zero for combined or empty characters.

detaileddescription.para:
This is a fast-path method for comparing a single-code point character, without the color.
The single code point, or 0 if this character is combined or empty.

briefdescription.para: Get the stored Unicode code points.
Get the number of stored Unicode code points.
Get the character color.
Get the character attributes.
Get the combined character style.
Get the display width on a terminal in cells.
Get the number of UTF-8 bytes needed to encode this character.
Append the UTF-8 representation to an existing buffer.

parameterdescription.para: The destination buffer.

briefdescription.para: Replace the full style of this character.

parameterdescription.para: The new style.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with an additional combining code point appended.

parameterdescription.para: The combining code point to append.
How invalid combining code points are handled.

simplesect.para: A copy of this character with the combining code point appended. EncodingErrors::Replace and EncodingErrors::Ignore keep the original character unchanged for invalid combining code points or when the storage is already full.

parameterdescription.para: If encodingErrors is Throw and the code point is unsupported.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with style applied on top of the stored style.

detaileddescription.para:
Inherited color components keep the current color component, and unspecified attributes keep the current attribute state.
style
The style override to apply.
A copy of this character with the overlaid style.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with the given color replacing the stored color.

parameterdescription.para: The replacement color.

simplesect.para: A copy of this character with exactly color.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with the given character style replaced.

parameterdescription.para: The replacement style.

simplesect.para: A copy of this character with exactly style.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with the given attributes replacing the stored attributes.

parameterdescription.para: The replacement attributes.

simplesect.para: A copy of this character with exactly attributes.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with style used as the base underneath the stored style.

detaileddescription.para:
The stored color and stored attributes overwrite the base style.
style
The base style.
A copy of this character resolved against the base style.

briefdescription.para: Create a character with another character used as the style base.

detaileddescription.para:
Only the style is used from base; the stored Unicode character is preserved.
base
The character providing the base color and attributes.
A copy of this character resolved against base.

briefdescription.para: Test if this character is empty (has no code-point).
Test if this character is spacing.

detaileddescription.para: Tests for space, tab, newline, and CR.

briefdescription.para: Test if this character is a control character.
Test if this character is one of the given characters.

detaileddescription.para: Tests only the character, not the color. Only tests one code-point characters.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Char &other, bool colorEnabled=true, bool attributeEnabled=true) const noexcept
memberdef.name: renderedEquals

briefdescription.para: Compare how two characters would appear on screen.

detaileddescription.para:
Code points must match exactly. When colorEnabled is true, inherited color components are treated as the terminal default color before comparing. When attributeEnabled is true, inherited attributes are treated as disabled before comparing.
other
The character to compare with.
colorEnabled
true to include colors in the comparison.
attributeEnabled
true to include character attributes in the comparison.
true if both characters render identically.

briefdescription.para: Get a hash for this character and its color.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Color color, const CharAttributes attributes) const noexcept
memberdef.name: withOverlay

memberdef.argsstring: (const Color color, const CharAttributes attributes) const noexcept
memberdef.name: withBase

briefdescription.para: A space with inherited colors.
A shared empty character with inherited colors.
Create an empty render cell that carries only the given style.

detaileddescription.para:
This is mainly used for wide-character continuation cells, which must stay logically empty while preserving the visible style of the leading cell.
style
The style to store on the empty block.
An empty block with style.

memberdef.argsstring: (const impl::CombinedChar character, const CharStyle style) noexcept
memberdef.name: Char

briefdescription.para: Represents a character string with combined terminal style information.

detaileddescription.para: Used by the UI code to render colored text blocks on the console.
